content.text: I found internal run transcripts that mention issue `#195`; I’m extracting the exact failure message from those so we can identify the real root cause, not just the label flips.

List PostgreSQL tables in ai database
input.command: docker exec agentos-db psql -U ai -d ai -c "SELECT schemaname, tablename FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname NOT IN ('pg_catalog','information_schema') ORDER BY schemaname, tablename;"

content.text: I’m checking persisted scheduler run records in Postgres now; that should give the exact failure payload even though the API container has already restarted.

input.description: Inspect columns for schedule run records
input.command: docker exec agentos-db psql -U ai -d ai -c "SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='ai' AND table_name='agno_schedule_runs' ORDER BY ordinal_position;"

input.description: Find recent schedule runs mentioning issue 195
input.command: docker exec agentos-db psql -U ai -d ai -c "SELECT id, schedule_id, status, to_timestamp(triggered_at/1000.0) AS triggered_at, left(error,200) AS error FROM ai.agno_schedule_runs WHERE output::text ILIKE '%issues/195%' OR error ILIKE '%195%' ORDER BY triggered_at DESC LIMIT 20;"

input.description: Inspect session table columns for run outputs
input.command: docker exec agentos-db psql -U ai -d ai -c "SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='ai' AND table_name='agno_sessions' ORDER BY ordinal_position;"

input.description: Search workflow sessions mentioning issue 195
input.command: docker exec agentos-db psql -U ai -d ai -c "SELECT session_id, workflow_id, to_timestamp(updated_at/1000.0) AS updated_at FROM ai.agno_sessions WHERE runs::text ILIKE '%issues/195%' OR workflow_data::text ILIKE '%issues/195%' ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 20;"

input.description: Count sessions mentioning issue 195 in JSON
input.command: docker exec agentos-db psql -U ai -d ai -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai.agno_sessions WHERE runs::text ILIKE '%issues/195%' OR workflow_data::text ILIKE '%issues/195%';"

input.description: Count matching sessions with unaligned output
input.command: docker exec agentos-db psql -U ai -d ai -tA -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai.agno_sessions WHERE runs::text ILIKE '%issues/195%' OR workflow_data::text ILIKE '%issues/195%';"

input.description: Verify psql output behavior with simple query
input.command: docker exec agentos-db psql -U ai -d ai -tA -c "SELECT 1;" && echo DONE

input.description: Test psql query with JSON text predicate
input.command: docker exec agentos-db psql -U ai -d ai -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai.agno_sessions WHERE runs IS NOT NULL;"

input.description: Count sessions where runs contains 195
input.command: docker exec agentos-db psql -U ai -d ai -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai.agno_sessions WHERE runs::text ILIKE '%195%';"

input.description: Get current labels on issue 195
Search pull requests referencing issue 195
Search repository PRs for references to issue 195

input.pattern: 'Function' object is not callable|Function\" object is not callable|object is not callable
Function' object is not callable|object is not callable

content.text:
Yes — it **was picked for coding**, and it failed in the coding executor path.

- `agentos-api` is currently up (`Up About a minute`), so container is healthy **now**.
- Issue `#195` timeline shows coding was attempted:
  - `rebaseai-working-coding` added at `2026-06-14T10:39:25Z`, then immediately replaced by `rebaseai-failed-coding`
  - retried at `2026-06-14T10:48:20Z`, failed again at `2026-06-14T10:48:23Z`
- Current labels on the issue confirm failed state: `rebaseai-designed`, `rebaseai-design-approved`, `rebaseai-failed-coding`.
- There is no coding PR for `#195` (only design PR `#196`), which means it died before PR creation.

Why it failed (root cause):
- The recorded dispatch error for this exact issue was: **`'Function' object is not callable`**.
- That means the workflow tried to invoke a wrapped Agno `Function` like a plain callable in the coding path.
- This is captured in the prior run transcript: [Coding failure fix thread](82a3118c-7689-4255-b2ad-2c638a8e88a0).

Important nuance:
- I could not pull the original failure stack from current container logs because the API container restarted recently, so old stdout logs are gone.
- But the GitHub label transitions + persisted transcript evidence are enough to conclude this was a real coding-dispatch runtime bug, not an eligibility/routing miss.

content.text:
Validation ran and passed end-to-end via `./scripts/validate.sh`:

- `uv sync --locked --extra dev`
- `uv lock --check`
- `ruff format --check` + `ruff check`
- `mypy`
- `pytest tests/unit -q` (`681 passed`)

Then I created and pushed this branch:

- `fix/project-board-poll-coding-dispatch`
- Remote: `origin/fix/project-board-poll-coding-dispatch`
- PR URL shortcut: [https://github.com/trilogy-group/ab-agno/pull/new/fix/project-board-poll-coding-dispatch](https://github.com/trilogy-group/ab-agno/pull/new/fix/project-board-poll-coding-dispatch)

Important: your working tree is still dirty (`MM/M` files), so those code changes are **not** on the remote yet (no commit was made in this step). If you want, I can commit these validated changes next and push that commit.

<user_query>
you did push
</user_query>

Correct — I **did push**, but only the branch pointer.

What got pushed:
- New remote branch: `origin/fix/project-board-poll-coding-dispatch`

What did **not** get pushed:
- Your local uncommitted changes (`MM/M` files), because Git cannot push uncommitted work.

So you now have a remote branch created and tracking, but no new commit content on it yet. If you want, I can commit the current validated changes and push that commit now.

fix(project-board-poll): make coding dispatch deterministic and callable-safe

Replace prompt-based board routing with deterministic label-driven dispatch and fix executor invocation to support Agno Function wrappers, so coding runs no longer fail with non-callable errors and skip outcomes are explicitly reported.
EOF
)" && git status --short --branch

input.description: Create GitHub pull request for branch
input.command:
gh pr create --base main --head fix/project-board-poll-coding-dispatch --title "fix(project-board-poll): deterministic routing and callable-safe coding dispatch" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- replace prompt-based project-board dispatch with deterministic label/state routing for design and coding handoffs
- fix executor invocation to support both raw async callables and Agno `Function` wrappers, preventing `'Function' object is not callable` failures in coding
- improve dispatch observability with explicit skip reasons/eligibility context and broaden tests around routing + wrapper execution paths

## Test plan
- [x] `./scripts/validate.sh`
- [x] pre-commit hooks on commit (`ruff`, `mypy`, formatting checks)
EOF
)"

@@

     assert out.success is True
-    issue_client.read_issue.assert_not_awaited()
+    assert issue_client.read_issue.await_count == 3
     run_design.assert_not_awaited()
     run_coder.assert_not_awaited()
@@
 async def test_dispatch_supports_function_wrapped_coder_executor() -> None:
@@
     processed = out.content["project_board_dispatch"]["processed"]
     assert processed[0]["action"] == "coding"
     assert processed[0]["ok"] is True
+
+
+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_dispatch_does_not_recoding_already_coded_issue() -> None:
+    changes = {"old_state": {}, "new_state": {"PVTI_1": _issue_snap(number=195)}}
+    bundle, issue_client, run_design, run_coder = _configure(changes)
+    issue_client.read_issue.return_value = {
+        "url": "https://github.com/trilogy-group/ab-agno/issues/195",
+        "title": "Already coded issue",
+        "body": "Issue body",
+        "created_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z",
+        "labels": [DESIGNED_LABEL, DESIGN_APPROVED_LABEL, CODED_LABEL],
+        "number": 195,
+        "state": "open",
+    }
+    detect_out = StepOutput(
+        content={
+            "project_board_poll": {
+                "has_changes": True,
+                "project_url": DEFAULT_PROJECT_URL,
+                "changes": changes,
+            },
+        },
+        success=True,
+    )
+    step_input = StepInput(
+        input={"project_url": DEFAULT_PROJECT_URL},
+        previous_step_outputs={"detect_project_changes": detect_out},
+    )
+    out = await bundle.dispatch_deterministic(step_input)
+
+    assert out.success is True
+    run_design.assert_not_awaited()
+    run_coder.assert_not_awaited()
+    skipped = out.content["project_board_dispatch"]["skipped"]
+    assert skipped[0]["issue_url"] == "https://github.com/trilogy-group/ab-agno/issues/195"
+    assert skipped[0]["reason"] == "already_coded"
+    assert skipped[0]["eligibility"] == {"coding": False, "design": False}
*** End Patch

content.text:
Done end-to-end.

- Committed and pushed to `fix/project-board-poll-coding-dispatch`
  - `505af9d` `fix(project-board-poll): make coding dispatch deterministic and callable-safe`
  - `1775bc9` `fix(project-board-poll): prevent re-coding loops and align cap source`
- PR created: [https://github.com/trilogy-group/ab-agno/pull/235](https://github.com/trilogy-group/ab-agno/pull/235)

Babysit status (completed first pass):
- CI is green: `conventional-pr-title`, `lint`, `type`, `test-unit`, `Semgrep` all passing.
- Greptile-raised issues were fixed in code and tests, then review threads were resolved.
- PR is mergeable and clean (`mergeable: MERGEABLE`, `mergeStateStatus: CLEAN`).
- Unresolved review threads: `0`.
- Working tree is clean and branch is fully pushed.

Remaining blocker to merge:
- No human approval/review decision yet (`reviewDecision` empty).
